properties.name: endpoint-gate.gates
properties.description: Per-gate configuration including enabled state and rollout percentage.

properties.name: endpoint-gate.gates.[*].enabled
properties.description: Whether this endpoint gate is enabled.

properties.name: endpoint-gate.gates.[*].rollout
properties.description: Rollout percentage for this endpoint gate (0-100). 100 means fully rolled out to all requests; 0 means no requests even if enabled.

properties.name: endpoint-gate.gates.[*].condition
properties.description: SpEL expression evaluated against the request context to conditionally enable this gate. Available variables: headers, params, cookies, path, method, remoteAddress. If empty, no condition is applied.

properties.name: endpoint-gate.response.type
properties.description: The type of response to return.

properties.name: endpoint-gate.default-enabled
properties.description: Whether undefined endpoint gates are enabled by default. Defaults to false (fail-closed): undefined gates block access. Set to true for fail-open behavior: undefined gates allow access.

properties.name: endpoint-gate.condition.fail-on-error
properties.description: Whether condition evaluation errors should cause access denial. Defaults to true (fail-closed): evaluation errors block access. Set to false for fail-open behavior: evaluation errors allow access.

properties.name: endpoint-gate.gates.[*].schedule.start
properties.description: The start date-time of the schedule window. The gate is treated as disabled before this time. If omitted, the gate has no start restriction.

properties.name: endpoint-gate.gates.[*].schedule.end
properties.description: The end date-time of the schedule window. The gate is treated as disabled after this time. If omitted, the gate has no end restriction.

properties.name: endpoint-gate.gates.[*].schedule.timezone
properties.description: The timezone used to evaluate start and end times. If omitted, the system default timezone is used.
